attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a pen in a black cup on a dresser near a computer.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a room with two primary pieces of furniture: a wooden dresser and a dark brown bookshelf-style side table, both situated on a light beige carpeted floor against a textured off-white wall. The lighting is ambient and even, with no strong shadows.

Relevant Objects and Their Types:

1.  Agent: A robotic agent is implied to be present but not visually depicted. It is initially not at any object's location.

2.  Receptacles:
    - Black Cup: A black, cylindrical cup with a handle, sitting on the top surface of the dark brown side table. It is a receptacle capable of holding small objects.
    - Wooden Dresser: A large, light brown wooden dresser with multiple drawers. The top surface serves as a location for placing objects, and the drawers are potential receptacles, though their openable status is not specified in the domain for this task. For this instruction, the top surface is treated as the relevant location.
    - Side Table: The dark brown, low bookshelf-style table is also a receptacle, with open shelves holding books and the top surface holding the cup and keys.

3.  Objects:
    - Pen: A yellow pen is visible on the top surface of the wooden dresser, to the right of a laptop.
    - Laptop: A grey laptop with a black screen is open and placed on the dresser. It is an object but not a receptacle.
    - Keys: A set of keys with a black fob is on the side table next to the black cup.
    - Books: Several books are stored in the shelves of the side table.
    - Radio/Alarm Clock: A brown, rounded object with a blue digital display (showing "03:51" in one view) sits on the side table. It is an object.
    - Lamp: A white lamp with a beige base is on the dresser next to the laptop.
    - Smartphone: A blue smartphone is on the dresser near the lamp.

Quantities:

- 1 Black Cup
- 1 Yellow Pen
- 1 Grey Laptop
- 1 Set of Keys
- Multiple Books (at least 4 visible in shelves)
- 1 Brown Radio/Alarm Clock
- 1 White Lamp
- 1 Blue Smartphone

Spatial Relationships:

- The yellow pen is on the top surface of the wooden dresser.
- The black cup is on the top surface of the dark brown side table.
- The laptop, lamp, and smartphone are also on the dresser, positioned near the pen.
- The keys and radio are on the side table, next to the black cup.
- The side table is positioned to the right of the dresser from the viewer's perspective in the top-down views.
- The agent is not currently at the location of the pen or the black cup.

Current State for Planning:

- The pen is not being held by the agent.
- The black cup is not being held by the agent.
- The pen is not in the black cup.
- The black cup is not openable or opened (as per the domain, it is a simple receptacle without an openable mechanism).
- The pen and cup are not toggled, sliced, cleaned, heated, or cooled, as these states are irrelevant to the task.
- The agent must first navigate to the pen's location on the dresser, then pick it up, then navigate to the black cup's location on the side table, and finally place the pen into the cup.

Visual Properties for Distinction:

- The pen is distinctly yellow.
- The cup is black with a visible handle.
- The dresser is light brown wood with visible drawer pulls.
- The side table is a darker brown with open shelving.
- The laptop is grey with a black screen.
- The radio has a prominent blue digital display.
- The lamp has a white conical shade and a beige base.
- The smartphone has a blue casing.

This description provides the necessary context for the robotic agent to plan the sequence of actions required to place the pen in the black cup.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_pen_in_cup)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        black_cup - receptacle
        dresser - object
        side_table - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        smartphone - object
        keys - object
        radio - object
        book1 book2 book3 book4 - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot pen))
        (not (atLocation robot black_cup))
        (not (atLocation robot dresser))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))

        ; Pen is on the dresser
        (atLocation pen dresser)

        ; Black cup is on the side table
        (atLocation black_cup side_table)

        ; Other objects are at their locations
        (atLocation laptop dresser)
        (atLocation lamp dresser)
        (atLocation smartphone dresser)
        (atLocation keys side_table)
        (atLocation radio side_table)
        (atLocation book1 side_table)
        (atLocation book2 side_table)
        (atLocation book3 side_table)
        (atLocation book4 side_table)

        ; Cup is not openable (as per domain, simple receptacle)
        (not (openable black_cup))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pen black_cup)
        )
    )
)
```

(define (problem put_pen_in_cup)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        black_cup - receptacle
        dresser - object
        side_table - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        smartphone - object
        keys - object
        radio - object
        book1 book2 book3 book4 - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot pen))
        (not (atLocation robot black_cup))
        (not (atLocation robot dresser))
        (not (atLocation robot side_table))

        ; Pen is on the dresser
        (atLocation pen dresser)

        ; Black cup is on the side table
        (atLocation black_cup side_table)

        ; Other objects are at their locations
        (atLocation laptop dresser)
        (atLocation lamp dresser)
        (atLocation smartphone dresser)
        (atLocation keys side_table)
        (atLocation radio side_table)
        (atLocation book1 side_table)
        (atLocation book2 side_table)
        (atLocation book3 side_table)
        (atLocation book4 side_table)

        ; Cup is not openable (as per domain, simple receptacle)
        (not (openable black_cup))

        ; Agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pen black_cup)
        )
    )
)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a pen in a black cup on a dresser near a computer.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a wooden dresser with a laptop, a pen, and a black cup on its surface. The dresser itself is a piece of furniture with multiple drawers and serves as a surface for placing objects. The laptop is gray with a black screen and is positioned on the right side of the dresser. A yellow pen lies near the laptop. A black cup, which is a receptacle capable of holding objects, is located on the left side of the dresser, near a lamp and a brown alarm clock.

The black cup is not currently holding any object. The pen is on the dresser surface, not inside the cup. The lamp is turned on, casting light on the scene. The alarm clock has a digital display showing "03:51" and is placed next to the cup. The laptop is closed and not powered on.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location and must first navigate to the dresser to interact with the pen or the cup. The black cup is a receptacle and can be used to hold the pen. The pen is an object that can be picked up and placed into the cup. The dresser is not a receptacle but serves as a surface where objects are placed.

There are no other receptacles visible in the scene that are relevant to the task. The goal is to place the pen inside the black cup, which requires the robot to first go to the dresser, pick up the pen, and then put it into the cup. The spatial relationship between the pen and the cup is that they are both on the dresser, with the pen to the right of the cup. The laptop is also on the dresser, to the right of the pen. The lamp and alarm clock are to the left of the cup. The robot must navigate to the dresser, pick up the pen, and then place it into the black cup.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_pen_in_cup)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        black_cup - receptacle
        dresser - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        alarm_clock - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot dresser)  ; robot starts at the dresser to interact with objects
        (not (holdsAny robot))      ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle pen black_cup))  ; pen is not in the cup
        (openable black_cup)        ; black cup is openable (assumed for placement)
        (not (opened black_cup))    ; black cup is not opened (not necessary for cup, but included for domain consistency)
        (isOn lamp)               ; lamp is on (from caption)
        (isToggled alarm_clock)     ; alarm clock is toggled (displaying time)
        ; pen is on dresser surface
        ; black_cup is on dresser surface
        ; laptop is on dresser surface
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pen black_cup)  ; pen is placed inside the black cup
    ))
)
```

        lamp - object
        alarm_clock - object
    )

        (not (holdsAny robot))      ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle pen black_cup))  ; pen is not in the cup
        (openable black_cup)        ; black cup is openable (assumed for placement)

        (isOn lamp)               ; lamp is on (from caption)
        (isToggled alarm_clock)     ; alarm clock is toggled (displaying time)
        ; pen is on dresser surface
        ; black_cup is on dresser surface
        ; laptop is on dresser surface
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pen black_cup)  ; pen is placed inside the black cup
    ))
)

;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

The black cup is not currently holding any object. The pen is on the dresser surface, not inside the cup. The lamp is turned on, casting light on the scene. The alarm clock has a digital display showing "03:51" and is placed next to the cup. The laptop is closed and not powered on.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location and must first navigate to the dresser to interact with the pen or the cup. The black cup is a receptacle and can be used to hold the pen. The pen is an object that can be picked up and placed into the cup. The dresser is not a receptacle but serves as a surface where objects are placed.

There are no other receptacles visible in the scene that are relevant to the task. The goal is to place the pen inside the black cup, which requires the robot to first go to the dresser, pick up the pen, and then put it into the cup. The spatial relationship between the pen and the cup is that they are both on the dresser, with the pen to the right of the cup. The laptop is also on the dresser, to the right of the pen. The lamp and alarm clock are to the left of the cup. The robot must navigate to the dresser, pick up the pen, and then place it into the black cup.

final.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_pen_in_cup)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        black_cup - receptacle
        dresser - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        alarm_clock - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot dresser)  ; robot starts at the dresser to interact with objects
        (not (holdsAny robot))      ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle pen black_cup))  ; pen is not in the cup
        (openable black_cup)        ; black cup is openable (assumed for placement)
        (not (opened black_cup))    ; black cup is not opened (not necessary for cup, but included for domain consistency)
        (isOn lamp)               ; lamp is on (from caption)
        (isToggled alarm_clock)     ; alarm clock is toggled (displaying time)
        ; pen is on dresser surface
        ; black_cup is on dresser surface
        ; laptop is on dresser surface
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pen black_cup)  ; pen is placed inside the black cup
    ))
)
```
final.problem:
(define (problem put_pen_in_cup)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        black_cup - receptacle
        dresser - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        alarm_clock - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot dresser)  ; robot starts at the dresser to interact with objects
        (not (holdsAny robot))      ; robot is not holding anything
        (not (inReceptacle pen black_cup))  ; pen is not in the cup
        (openable black_cup)        ; black cup is openable (assumed for placement)
        (not (opened black_cup))    ; black cup is not opened (not necessary for cup, but included for domain consistency)
        (isOn lamp)               ; lamp is on (from caption)
        (isToggled alarm_clock)     ; alarm clock is toggled (displaying time)
        ; pen is on dresser surface
        ; black_cup is on dresser surface
        ; laptop is on dresser surface
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pen black_cup)  ; pen is placed inside the black cup
    ))
)
final.plan:
(gotolocation robot pen)
(pickupobject robot pen)
(gotolocation robot black_cup)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot pen black_cup)
